title: Chapter 3 Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of flow control in Dart!

questions.explanation: 5 > 3 is true, so 'yes' is printed.

questions.question: In a switch statement, you can omit break and execution will continue to the next case.
questions.explanation: Yes, fall-through is allowed; you can use `continue` to explicitly jump to another case.

questions.question: The keyword to exit a loop immediately is _____.
questions.explanation: `break` terminates the loop.
Loop runs for i=0,1,2 → three times.

questions.question: A do-while loop always executes at least once.
questions.explanation: Yes, the condition is checked after the body.
Start x=1; first iteration: x=3; second: x=5; condition x<5 false, exit. Prints 5.

questions.question: The keyword to skip the rest of the current loop iteration is _____.
questions.explanation: `continue` jumps to the next iteration.

questions.question: A switch statement can be used with double values.
questions.explanation: Switch works with int, String, and enum types, not double.
Loop stops when i==2, so prints 0 and 1.

questions.question: In a for-in loop, the variable takes each _____ of the collection.
questions.explanation: For-in iterates over elements.

questions.question: The condition in an if statement must be a boolean expression.
questions.explanation: Yes, unlike some languages where integers can be used.
Executes once (prints 0) then condition false.

questions.question: The _____ case in a switch handles values not matched by other cases.
questions.explanation: `default` is the catch-all.

questions.question: Using continue in a for loop skips the increment step.
questions.explanation: Continue skips the rest of the body but the increment still happens at the end of iteration.
When j=1, continue skips print, so prints only when j=0: i=0→00, i=1→10, i=2→20.

questions.question: A loop that never ends is called an _____ loop.
questions.explanation: Infinite loop.

questions.question: You can use a switch statement to compare strings.
questions.explanation: Yes, switch works with strings (case values must be compile-time constants).
First condition true, prints A; else if is skipped.

questions.question: The _____ loop is best when you know the number of iterations in advance.
questions.explanation: For loop is ideal for counted iteration.

questions.question: Break can only be used inside loops, not in switch.
questions.explanation: Break is used in both loops and switch cases to exit.
i=0: print 0, i=1; i=1: print 1, i=2, break exits loop. Prints 0 1.

questions.question: To explicitly fall through to the next case in a switch, use the _____ keyword followed by a label.
questions.explanation: Dart uses `continue` with a label to fall through.

questions.question: The condition in a while loop is evaluated before each iteration.
questions.explanation: Yes, that's the definition of a pretest loop.

questions.question: The _____ statement can be used to return a value from a function and exit it.
questions.explanation: `return` exits the function, optionally with a value.
